description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges. The front panel features a cream-colored faceplate with two large dials, a horizontal frequency indicator, and a speaker grille with two circular speaker areas. The wood casing is dark and shows signs of wear, including visible smudges and handprints, giving it an aged, possibly neglected appearance. The dials and faceplate are also marked with red stains, adding to the distressed look. The back panel is plain and dark, likely made of a different material, and includes a few small ports or screws. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, with tactile controls and a prominent brand label reading 'henle' on the front.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a cream faceplate, two large dials, a frequency indicator, and a speaker grille, all showing signs of wear and red stains.
description_view_0: The left side of the radio, showing the dark wooden casing with visible wear and a partial view of a control knob protruding from the far side.
description_view_1: The front of the radio, displaying the cream faceplate, two large dials, a horizontal frequency indicator, speaker grille, and the 'henle' brand label, with red stains and signs of aging.
description_view_2: The right side of the radio, similar to the left, with the dark wooden casing and a partial view of a control knob on the near side.
description_view_3: The back of the radio, featuring a plain, dark panel with a few small ports or screws and some red stains, lacking any controls or branding.
